summary: Batch of 7 testing hygiene fixes. All changes are test-only and generally sound: fake timer leak fixed with try/finally, real timing delay removed, clearAllMocks added, test file moved to __tests__/, I/O dependency documented, and test:unit script added. Two concerns: the auth-server timing test replacement is semantically weaker than the original, and the clearAllMocks addition is redundant given the existing restoreAllMocks plus fetchSpy reassignment.

suggestions.description: Re-awaiting an already-resolved promise does not verify the timeout actually fired and was properly guarded by the settled flag
suggestions.rationale: The original test waited 150ms (longer than the 100ms timeoutMs) to let the real setTimeout fire, confirming the settled-flag guard in auth-server.ts line 57-61 works. The new test just re-awaits the promise, which is trivially true for any resolved promise and does not exercise the timeout path at all. The test name says it does not reject after successful token even if timeout is short but the test no longer actually waits for the timeout to fire.
suggestions.suggestion: Either keep a short real delay (e.g. 50ms above the 100ms timeout to let the setTimeout fire), or use fake timers to advance past the timeout and then verify the promise is still resolved. Alternatively, acknowledge the test is weaker and update the comment to reflect what it actually verifies.

suggestions.description: vi.clearAllMocks() is redundant when afterEach already calls vi.restoreAllMocks() and beforeEach reassigns fetchSpy
suggestions.rationale: vi.restoreAllMocks() resets all mocks to their original implementation AND clears call history. Additionally, fetchSpy is reassigned to a fresh vi.fn() on the very next line (line 9). Adding clearAllMocks before both of these operations has no observable effect. It is harmless but suggests a misunderstanding of the mock lifecycle.
suggestions.suggestion: Either remove the clearAllMocks call since it is fully superseded by restoreAllMocks + fresh vi.fn(), or remove restoreAllMocks from afterEach and rely solely on clearAllMocks in beforeEach (less common but valid). Pick one pattern, not both.

suggestions.description: try/finally works but diverges from the suggested fix approach of beforeEach/afterEach
suggestions.rationale: The fix document specified moving useFakeTimers to beforeEach and useRealTimers to afterEach. The try/finally approach achieves the same leak-prevention goal and is arguably better since only one test needs fake timers. This is not a defect, but the deviation from spec should be noted. The implementation choice is sound.
suggestions.suggestion: No action needed. The try/finally approach is a valid alternative that avoids unnecessarily applying fake timers to all tests in the describe block.
